subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: A commercial water distributor supplies an office with gallons of water once a week. Suppose that the weekly supplies in tens of gallons is a random variable with pdf $f(x) = \begin{cases} 5(1-x)^4 & 0 < x < 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ Approximately, how many gallons should be delivered in one week so that the probability of the supply is 0.1?
